form_analysis.form_header_text: FY27 Programmatic & Language Appropriations Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
PLEASE READ ALL INSTRUCTIONS IN THIS FORM CAREFULLY. EACH YEAR REP. FRANKLIN IS UNABLE TO VET SPECIFIC REQUESTS DUE TO INCOMPLETE/INACCURATE SUBMISSIONS. DO NOT SUBMIT YOUR REQUEST TO THE INCORRECT FORM. 

Instructions:

1. This form is for programmatic and language requests only.
IMPORTANT: THIS FORM IS NOT FOR DEFENSE REQUESTS. For all Defense Appropriation requests, please use this form: https://forms.gle/XWxNLimd8vVYeyrs6
IMPORTANT: THIS FORM IS NOT FOR COMMUNITY PROJECT FUNDING (CPF) REQUESTS. DO NOT SUBMIT A CPF (EARMARK) REQUEST TO THIS FORM. For all CPF requests, please use this form: https://forms.gle/gRUomTyJYtyRRbfd8
3. Deadlines:
The deadline for all requests is March 6th, 2026. 
Deadline is subject to change once guidelines from the Appropriations Committee are released.
4. All required questions on the form must be completed. 

5. Word limits on request descriptions and language are set by the Appropriations Committee, whose electronic filing system won't accept larger submissions.

fields.rationale: The request is for a specific funding amount for NASA's Science Mission Directorate, which is a programmatic funding request.

fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA appropriations are handled by the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee.

fields.rationale: The account is the Science Mission Directorate within NASA.

fields.label: Amount for this Programmatic Request:
fields.rationale: This matches the target funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Amount of Proposed Increase/Decrease Funding Level:
fields.rationale: This represents the net increase requested over the FY2026 enacted level of $7.250 billion.

fields.rationale: The reference values state that the President's Budget Request for FY2027 has not yet been released.

fields.rationale: This is the enacted total for FY2026 as per H.R. 6938.

fields.rationale: This is the FY2025 enacted funding level for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Enacted funding level in FY24:
fields.rationale: The FY2024 enacted amount was not provided in the authoritative funding history context.

fields.label: Enacted funding level for FY20 (Pre-COVID Spending)
fields.rationale: The FY2020 enacted amount was not provided in the authoritative funding history context.

fields.label: Description of this Programmatic request (500 characters or less):
fields.draft_value:
* Request $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate
* Restore stability to the aerospace workforce and mission schedules
* Support planetary exploration, Earth science, and astrophysics priorities
* Maintain U.S. leadership in the face of rising international competition
fields.rationale: Derived from the authoritative funding ask ($9B) and the core objectives of the Science Mission Directorate found in the advocacy context.

fields.label: Please describe the problem or issue this Programmatic request will address:
fields.draft_value:
* Funding volatility and proposed FY 2026 cuts disrupted critical mission roadmaps
* Significant pressure on the specialized aerospace workforce and industrial base
* Rapidly expanding space science ambitions from international competitors like China
* Delays to peer-reviewed Decadal Survey scientific priorities
fields.rationale: Draws on SMD topline history (FY26 volatility) and advocacy talking points regarding workforce and China.

fields.label: Please describe the goals and/or expected outcome of this Programmatic request:
fields.draft_value:
* Ensure execution of National Academies Decadal Survey priority missions
* Strengthen the STEM talent pipeline and national innovation workforce
* Set international norms for 21st-century exploration through sustained leadership
* Deliver transformative results in solar system discovery and Earth observation
fields.rationale: Focuses on the Decadal Survey and workforce goals highlighted in the advocacy points and tone guide.

fields.label: Please number the priority of this Programmatic request (if your organization is submitting more than one request, please list the priority of this request):
fields.rationale: Priority level is internal to the submitter's organization and not available in the provided context.

sections.header: National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs Additional Supplemental Questions

fields.label: For National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs requests only, please describe how this request is in the national security interest of the United States (if non-applicable, answer N/A):
fields.draft_value: * Critical front in the space race to set exploration norms for the 21st century\n* Maintenance of the specialized aerospace industrial base and technical workforce\n* Advancing U.S. technological leadership to counter expansion by competitors like China
fields.rationale: Based on advocacy talking points linking space science to national competition and industrial base stability.

fields.label: Have you submitted this request to Rep. Franklin's Office in the past?
fields.rationale: Auto-corrected: 'null' is not a valid option.

fields.label: Has this request been included in any previous appropriations bills? Including House or Senate Versions that did not become law. If yes, please provide details on what was included.
fields.draft_value: * NASA Science is an established program funded in successive CJS appropriations\n* FY 2026 Enacted: $7.250 billion (H.R. 6938)\n* FY 2025 Enacted: $7.334 billion
fields.rationale: Cites exact enacted totals and bill numbers from the SMD topline funding history and fact sheet.

fields.label: Please list any other House Members you are submitting this request to (if only Rep. Franklin, answer N/A):
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: The reference values indicate these members are leads on an existing multi-member letter for this request.

fields.label: Please list any Senators you are submitting this request to (if non-applicable, answer N/A):
fields.rationale: The context specifies that there is no existing letter lead for the Senate yet.

fields.label: Project Justification: Please explain in a few sentences on how this Programmatic or Language request will help your organization, community, FL Congressional District 18, the State of Florida, and/or the nation:
fields.draft_value:
* $3 economic return for every $1 invested in NASA programs
* Bolsters the national aerospace industrial base and innovation workforce
* Sustains U.S. preeminence in scientific discovery and exploration prestige
* Inspires the next generation of Florida's STEM professionals and entrepreneurs
fields.rationale: Leverages the 3:1 ROI talking point and national workforce impact, as there is no specific NASA Science spending in FL-18.

fields.label: Appropriate Use: Please explain in a few sentences why any federal resources used to carry out this Programmatic or Language request would be an appropriate constitutional use of taxpayer resources:
fields.draft_value: * Fundamental public responsibility codified in Title 51 of the U.S. Code\n* Advances science, security, and competitiveness in ways the private sector cannot\n* High-risk, high-reward exploration that delivers broad public and scientific benefits
fields.rationale: Directly uses the advocacy talking point regarding Title 51 and the unique public responsibility of space science.
